description: Rewrite docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with comprehensive production-grade architecture documentation including a Mermaid diagram for the Event-Driven Sleep Audio Pipeline
steps: Read the existing docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to understand current skeleton structure

Rewrite docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with the following sections in order: (1) Title and High-Level Overview describing the full pipeline purpose and event-driven approach, (2) Mermaid diagram using 'flowchart TD' syntax showing: Input S3 Bucket, EventBridge rule, Step Functions state machine, Amazon Polly integration, optional Bedrock integration, metadata extraction/validation Lambda, Output S3 Bucket (with versioning), DynamoDB metadata table, SNS notifications (success and error), CloudWatch Logs/Alarms, and IAM roles. Label all arrows with event flow descriptions. (3) Data Flow section with detailed step-by-step explanation of the pipeline from upload to notification, (4) AWS Services section listing each service (S3 input/output, EventBridge, Step Functions, Lambda, Polly, Bedrock, DynamoDB, SNS, CloudWatch, IAM, KMS) with rationale for why each is chosen, (5) Security section covering least-privilege IAM roles, encryption at rest (KMS for S3 and DynamoDB), private buckets (no public access), VPC considerations, (6) Observability section covering CloudWatch Logs for all Lambda/Step Functions, CloudWatch Alarms for failures and latency, SNS notifications for pipeline completion/errors, X-Ray tracing consideration, (7) Cost Considerations section discussing pay-per-use model, S3 lifecycle policies, DynamoDB on-demand capacity, Lambda memory/timeout optimization, (8) Multi-Environment Support section explaining CDK context-based dev/stage/prod environments and how configuration varies per environment, (9) Future Extensibility section noting possible additions like real-time streaming, additional AI models, user preference APIs, analytics dashboards, (10) TDD Approach section (kept from existing doc) explaining the red-green-refactor cycle with CDK Assertions

Ensure the Mermaid diagram renders correctly on GitHub by using only standard flowchart TD syntax with simple node labels (no special characters that break rendering), proper arrow syntax (-->), and subgraphs for logical groupings (Processing, Storage, Notifications, Observability)
Verify consistency between the Mermaid diagram and the prose descriptions - every component in the diagram must be mentioned in the text and vice versa
acceptance_criteria: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md contains a comprehensive Mermaid diagram using 'flowchart TD' syntax with all major AWS resources labeled
The diagram includes: Input S3 Bucket, EventBridge, Step Functions, Lambda functions (validation, Polly TTS, optional Bedrock), Output S3 Bucket, DynamoDB, SNS (success/error topics), CloudWatch
All diagram arrows are labeled with event flow descriptions
The document has dedicated sections for Security, Observability, Cost Considerations, and Multi-Environment Support
Amazon Polly and Bedrock are explicitly mentioned with their roles in the pipeline
SNS notifications for both success and error paths are documented
Output S3 bucket with versioning enabled is documented
The document is self-consistent - diagram matches prose
No CDK code changes are made
verification: Read the final docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and confirm all required sections exist
Confirm the Mermaid code block starts with ```mermaid and uses flowchart TD syntax
Confirm no files outside docs/ were modified (except AGENT_GUIDELINES.md in root or docs/)
Run a quick markdown syntax check by searching for unclosed code blocks or broken links
findings: Documentation-only change completed successfully. All 10 required sections present. Mermaid diagram uses flowchart TD with subgraphs for Ingestion, Processing, Storage, Notifications, Observability, and Security. All arrows labeled. No code files modified.
